name: Close obsolete PR (target SHA stale)
description: When a PR's only substantive change is to set a workflow `uses:` SHA, and main now carries a NEWER SHA at the same location, the PR's edit would REGRESS main. Close-as-superseded with the canonical comment template — do NOT rebase (the rebase would revert main's progress).
match: PR diff adds `uses: ...@<old-sha>` but main has `uses: ...@<newer-sha>` at the same path+key

discovery.first_observed_in: 6 estate PRs in single session (somethings-fishy#26, nextgen-languages#55, stapeln#74, hypatia#349, typed-wasm#75, +1)
discovery.root_cause: Two reusable-pin-bump PRs land out of order. The first updates main to SHA-B; the second PR (filed earlier against SHA-A) still proposes SHA-A→SHA-A_renamed_to_B which is regressive vs main's current SHA-B+.
